rom_size: Make your Android to a Router!
desc:
OpenWRT 24.10 is a highly extensible Linux operating system for embedded devices and routers. This version includes the Realtek USB ethernet driver and configures eth0 as WAN by default.

Key Features:
• Built-in Realtek USB ethernet driver
• Default eth0 WAN configuration
• Turns your Android device into a functional router
• Highly customizable networking environment

Repository & Documentation:
https://openwrt.org/
creator: Vectras Team

rom_size: Best light-weight linux for Vectras VM
desc:
Alpine Linux is a security-oriented, lightweight Linux distribution based on musl libc and busybox. It is designed for resource efficiency and security.

Key Features:
• Built on musl libc and busybox
• Highly secure and resource-efficient
• Ideal lightweight Linux for Vectras VM
• Fast boot times and low system overhead

Repository & Documentation:
https://alpinelinux.org/
creator: Vectras Team

desc:
KolibriOS is a tiny yet incredibly powerful and fast operating system that requires only a few megabytes of disk space and 8MB of RAM to run. (From Nomis_official, reuploaded by @tokaevUser).

Key Features:
• Incredibly fast and lightweight requiring minimal RAM (8MB)
• Includes word processor, image viewer, web browser, and over 30 games
• Full FAT12/16/32 support with read-only NTFS, exFAT, ISO9660, and Ext2/3/4
• Includes drivers for popular sound, network, and graphics cards

Repository & Documentation:
https://kolibrios.org/
creator: Nomis_official & tokaevUser

desc:
ReactOS is a free and open-source operating system based on the Windows NT architecture, aiming to provide a binary-compatible alternative to Microsoft Windows. (From @SilentGamer191).

Key Features:
• Binary-compatible with Microsoft Windows applications and drivers
• Lightweight and efficient for modern and older hardware
• Familiar Windows XP-like interface
• Default Login - User: Vectras / Password: 1234567890

Repository & Documentation:
https://reactos.org/
creator: SilentGamer191

desc:
MS-DOS (Microsoft Disk Operating System) is a classic command-line operating system for x86-based personal computers, widely used during the 1980s and early 1990s before the advent of GUIs.

Key Features:
• Ultra-light, classic command-line interface
• Perfect for running vintage DOS games and applications
• Historically significant operating system by Microsoft
• Low overhead and immediate execution environment

Repository & Documentation:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MS-DOS
creator: Nguyen Bao An Bui

rom_size: The operating system is too light
desc:
OS/2 is a classic proprietary computer operating system initially developed jointly by IBM and Microsoft as a replacement for DOS. (From marstraveler01).

Key Features:
• Historical operating system with a unique graphical user interface
• Represents a pivotal era in computing history (1987-2001)
• Extremely lightweight by modern standards
• Ideal for retro computing and running legacy OS/2 software

Repository & Documentation:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OS/2
creator: marstraveler01

desc:
Debian GNU/Linux is a free and open-source Linux distribution developed by the Debian Project. It is one of the oldest and most stable operating systems based on the Linux kernel.

Key Features:
• Highly stable and reliable base system
• Foundation for many popular Linux distributions
• Lightweight and highly customizable environment
• Default Login - User: root / Password: [None]

Repository & Documentation:
https://www.debian.org/
creator: steven

desc:
Tiny Core Linux is the recommended standard version for users who want a fast, minimal graphical user interface. It builds upon the base Core system by adding X/GUI extensions, providing a dynamic FLTK/FLWM desktop environment right out of the box.

Key Features:
• Minimalist FLTK/FLWM Graphical Desktop
• Incredibly fast boot times and ultra-low RAM usage
• Ideal for wired network connections to download additional extensions

Repository & Documentation:
http://tinycorelinux.net/
creator: Vectras Team

desc:
Core Linux (often referred to as Micro Core) is the absolute base system, offering a pure command-line interface. It is exceptionally lightweight and designed for advanced users who want to build a custom system from the ground up without any unnecessary overhead.

Key Features:
• Command-Line Interface only (No GUI)
• Extremely minimal footprint (usually under 20MB)
• Maximum customizability for server or custom embedded use

Repository & Documentation:
http://tinycorelinux.net/
creator: Vectras Team

desc:
CorePlus Linux is the most comprehensive installation image in the Tiny Core family. It includes the base system and installation tools, plus advanced hardware support out-of-the-box. This is the ideal choice for setting up a machine that requires immediate Wi-Fi access or alternative desktop environments.

Key Features:
• Choice of Multiple Window Managers (FLWM, JWM, IceWM, Fluxbox, Hackedbox, Openbox)
• Built-in Wireless (Wi-Fi) network support
• Non-US keyboard layouts included

Repository & Documentation:
http://tinycorelinux.net/
creator: Vectras Team

desc:
SliTaz OS is an ultra-lightweight Linux distribution designed to deliver a fast, complete desktop experience while using minimal system resources. It is ideal for older computers, virtual machines, rescue environments, and lightweight everyday use.

Key Features:
• Lightweight graphical desktop (Openbox by default)
• Very small footprint (typically around 50–60MB ISO)
• Fast boot time with low RAM and CPU usage
• Includes package manager, web browser, and essential utilities

Repository & Documentation:
https://slitaz.org/
creator: Vectras Team

desc:
Damn Small Linux is an ultra-lightweight Linux distribution created to provide a complete desktop operating system while remaining extremely small and efficient. It is an excellent choice for legacy hardware, virtual machines, portable environments, and users seeking maximum performance with minimal resource usage.

Key Features:
• Lightweight Fluxbox desktop environment
• Extremely small footprint with fast boot times
• Includes web browser, text editor, multimedia tools, and essential utilities
• Ideal for reviving older PCs and low-resource virtual machines

Repository & Documentation:
https://www.damnsmalllinux.org/
creator: Vectras Team
